item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from the start and remain opposed through the final turn. Agent A briefly hardens from a rights-first rejection into stronger language about society-first claims enabling abuse, then returns to a position allowing narrow public-interest exceptions. Agent B similarly hardens in the middle but ends with the same narrow-exception rights-first position. No visible flip or directional drift.
